expectations.text: Tri-driver pattern: auto-detects Neon serverless vs Neon HTTP vs local pg based on URL and NEXT_RUNTIME
expectations.evidence: server/db/index.ts only uses @neondatabase/serverless with neon-http driver. No detection of URL scheme or NEXT_RUNTIME, no conditional logic for selecting between Neon serverless WebSocket, Neon HTTP, or local pg. It hardcodes a single driver: `const sql = neon(process.env.DATABASE_URL); export const db = drizzle(sql, { schema });`

expectations.text: Lazy proxy pattern: db instance doesn't eagerly connect on import
expectations.evidence: server/db/index.ts eagerly creates the neon connection and drizzle instance at module scope: `const sql = neon(process.env.DATABASE_URL); export const db = drizzle(sql, { schema });`. There is no lazy proxy, no Proxy object, and no deferred initialization. The db connects immediately on import.

expectations.text: casing: 'snake_case' set in BOTH drizzle.config.ts AND client initialization
expectations.evidence: drizzle.config.ts has no casing option: `defineConfig({ schema, out, dialect, dbCredentials })`. The drizzle client initialization in server/db/index.ts also has no casing option: `drizzle(sql, { schema })`. While the schema manually uses snake_case column names (e.g., `created_at`), the explicit `casing: 'snake_case'` configuration is absent from both config files.

Three files exist at services/database/ path: packages--nextjs--services--database--api.ts, packages--nextjs--services--database--hooks.ts, packages--nextjs--services--database--index.ts. These provide the client-side API wrapper, react-query hooks, and barrel export.

API routes in app/api/users/route.ts and app/api/users/[id]/route.ts directly call `db.select().from(users)`, `db.insert(users)`, `db.update(users)`, `db.delete(users)` inline. There is no separate repository class or module that abstracts the database queries. The services/database/ layer is a client-side fetch wrapper, not a server-side repository pattern.

expectations.text: Root package.json has proxy scripts (drizzle-kit, db:seed, db:wipe)
expectations.evidence: No root package.json file exists in the outputs. The nextjs package.json has db:generate, db:migrate, db:push, db:studio scripts but no db:seed or db:wipe. There are no proxy scripts at the root level, and no seed/wipe functionality is present at all.

expectations.text: Docker Compose for local PostgreSQL development
expectations.evidence: No docker-compose.yml or any Docker-related file exists in the outputs. The summary.md setup instructions only mention using Neon cloud: 'Create a Neon PostgreSQL database at https://neon.tech'. No local PostgreSQL development option is provided.

The .env.example file instructs users to 'copy this file, rename it to .env.local'. The summary.md also says: 'Copy the connection string to packages/nextjs/.env.local as DATABASE_URL'. There is no .env.development file or reference to it.

No reference to PRODUCTION_DATABASE_HOSTNAME anywhere in the outputs. The server/db/index.ts only checks if DATABASE_URL is set: `if (!process.env.DATABASE_URL) { throw new Error(...) }`. No hostname validation or production safety mechanism exists.

expectations.text: All required dependencies in correct locations (drizzle-orm, @neondatabase/serverless, pg, dotenv, drizzle-kit, drizzle-seed, tsx, @types/pg)
expectations.evidence: packages/nextjs/package.json has drizzle-orm (dependency), @neondatabase/serverless (dependency), and drizzle-kit (devDependency). Missing: pg, dotenv, drizzle-seed, tsx, @types/pg. Only 3 of the 8 required packages are present.

suggestions: The implementation is a basic Drizzle + Neon HTTP setup without the advanced patterns expected by the evaluation. It lacks the tri-driver pattern, lazy proxy, snake_case config, repository pattern, Docker support, and production safety guards.
Without the skill file guidance, the agent defaulted to the simplest possible Neon integration - a single hardcoded neon-http driver with no flexibility for different environments.
The services/database/ convention was followed, which shows the agent understood some SE-2 patterns, but the server-side architecture is minimal.
The agent should have been prompted or guided to consider local development (Docker/pg), production safety, and proper configuration patterns.
eval_feedback.overall: Very poor score (1/10). The output is a functional but minimal Drizzle + Neon integration that only meets the services/database/ path convention. It completely lacks the sophisticated patterns expected: tri-driver auto-detection, lazy proxy, explicit snake_case casing config, repository abstraction, root proxy scripts, Docker Compose, .env.development usage, production safety guards, and several required dependencies. This demonstrates the significant gap between a naive implementation and the skill-guided approach.
